heading: Temporary assignment of excess real property
label: 526 Temporary assignment of excess real property
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)52640:483(g)., title II, § 202(g), .June 30, 1949, ch. 28863 Stat. 385In subsection (a), the words “for use as office or storage space or for a related purpose” are substituted for “for office, storage, or related facilities” for clarity.
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)52640:483(g)., title II, § 202(g), .June 30, 1949, ch. 28863 Stat. 385In subsection (a), the words “for use as office or storage space or for a related purpose” are substituted for “for office, storage, or related facilities” for clarity.

children.heading: .—Assignment of Space
children.label: a .—Assignment of Space
children.text: The Administrator of General Services may temporarily assign or reassign space in excess real property to a federal agency, for use as office or storage space or for a related purpose, if the Administrator determines that assignment or reassignment is more advantageous than permanent transfer. The Administrator shall determine the duration of the assignment or reassignment.

children.heading: .—Reimbursement for Maintenance
children.label: b .—Reimbursement for Maintenance
children.text: If there is no appropriation available to the Administrator for the expense of maintaining the space, the Administrator may obtain appropriate reimbursement from the federal agency.
